Build dry-run verification (the analyst applied both find/replace patches to a temp copy and ran the build, then restored the original):
  - cp build-scripts/generate-html.js /tmp/orig.js → apply FC1 (HTML hero swap) → apply FC2 (CSS rule insertion) → cp back to build-scripts → node build.js html → 1318.3 KB docs/index.html generated successfully.

Audit conclusion for HNOTE Item 2 (verdict-bar redundancy): NO CODE CHANGE NEEDED. The verdict bar chart from EXP-209 IS rendered on Overview at line 1364 of generate-html.js, ABOVE the four-up sc-hero. The four-up sc-hero (lines 1366–1383) is NOT a verdict-distribution view — it is a high-level scorecard (Claimed Wins / Actual Wins / Failures / Timestamp Error). No six-tile verdict-distribution display exists anywhere on Overview. The reviewer 6-adjacent-stat-tiles description was likely a misread of the four-up scorecard widget. Item 1 hero swap removes the four-up tiles entirely, automatically eliminating any residual reviewer ambiguity.

Live-count handling: The thesis sentence uses the existing dome-claim-count template variable (counts.total). counts.total is computed at build time from baseWins.filter(w=>/^[0-9]{3}$/.test(w.id)) — today this returns 69, matching the operator example wording in the HNOTE exactly. Going forward, as the dome adds new claims, the sentence updates automatically. This honors both (a) operator "treat as final" directive on the sentence wording (no editorialization of meaning) and (b) project rule "Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data" (CLAUDE.md). HNOTE verification clause "no derived content" is interpreted as "no editorial drift" not "no template substitution."

Eclipse badge link target: #p4-426 (section 4.2.6 "What would change our verdict") — verified present in data/sections.json part4 today AND verified to remain at the same id after EXP-254 (4.2.7 geometric trilemma) integrates. EXP-254 inserts 4.2.7 AFTER 4.2.6, not before; 4.2.6 keeps its eclipse role under both states.

Counterargument considered (per user preference): defender could argue the round-1 dashboard-feel was load-bearing and removing it reverses a previous deliberate design decision under round-2 designer pressure without independent justification. Rebuttal: the operator HNOTE explicitly elevates this from Phase C to active work and gives the rationale (the four-up tiles "don't add to a coherent thesis"; the actual thesis belongs at display scale). Round-2 designer praise is independent confirmation, not the cause. The round-1 deferral was conditional on credibility threshold not yet being crossed; round-2 establishes that threshold has been crossed (C+ → B+). Curmudgeon will stress-test this further.

Kernel of truth (per analyst directive): the round-1 dashboard treatment WAS load-bearing for the credibility lift from C+ to B+. We acknowledge that. Why kernel does not break the swap: round-1 failure mode (numbers not up-front) is now addressed by THREE other surfaces (verdict bar above, body-row figures within, accuracy-math-section below) — the dashboard single point of work has been distributed across multiple contextually-appropriate locations. The swap is a hierarchy reweighting, not a content deletion.

Output: EXP-253 — wholesale rewrite of §4.2.2 leading with dome's explicit misstatement of standard-physics eclipse prediction (claims globe predicts 0 nT). Two new leading paragraphs quote dome's live-power Domain 11 falsification criterion ("Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction)") and eclipse countdown banner ("globe predicts 0 nT") verbatim — both verified live at https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html on 2026-04-26 via curl; both byte-stable with archived form in raw-text/08-live-power.txt (lines 104 and 190, committed 2026-04-09 in fb79e4e). Existing Chapman 1933 / Meza et al. 2021 / 207-observations / 4-eclipse INTERMAGNET-study content preserved verbatim, demoted from lead to support. Closing paragraph delivers the BOU-2017-already-falsifies hammer with explicit trilemma framing: |−10.9| / 3.0 = 3.6× outside the dome's own ±3 nT "globe" envelope, so the dome must either accept its falsification rule (and concede globe-correct) or revise it (and concede non-discriminating). Anchor link forward to §4.2.5 for the input chain.

Secondary patch on §4.2.3: one-sentence cross-reference at the head of the opening paragraph naming the dependency on §4.2.2's misstatement framing ("only reads as a discrimination test because the dome misstates the globe prediction as zero"). Addresses the HNOTE INTEGRATION NOTES item 4 verbatim.

TLDR rewritten from Chapman-only 38-word two-sentence summary to misstatement-led 85-word three-sentence summary: (a) names misstatement, (b) contrasts dome's 0 vs Chapman's 5–25, (c) notes BOU 2017 already falsifies. All three sentences load-bearing.

Substring uniqueness verified for both patches (primary p4-422 full-block: 1 occurrence; secondary p4-423 leading sentence: 1 occurrence). Dry-run patch application produces clean HTML; new <details id="p4-422"> renders correctly within Part 4 tab structure.

Decider routing: queue-for-curmudgeon-then-integrate per HNOTE explicit routing. Curmudgeon recheck list embedded in the proposal: (1) re-verify dome quotes at review time (drift defense in place if dome edits live page), (2) test whether "globe predicts 0" framing is universal across dome material or limited to Domain 11, (3) test BOU-already-falsifies hammer for over-reach (trilemma already covers the obvious dome defenses), (4) optional NOAA/USGS Boulder primary-source cross-check on −10.9 nT.

NOT WORKED THIS RUN (deferred per one-item-per-run convention):

  HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 — five-item overview rework EXP commission (hero swap, distribution-bar audit, lightweight falsification module, How-to-Evaluate promotion, eclipse badge); deferred to next analyst run.

Per user-preferences directive (consider opposing arguments): the strongest defender objection is that "globe predicts 0" is reader-shorthand for "no eclipse-specific anomaly above background" rather than a literal claim about Chapman — i.e. we are uncharitably reading shorthand as a substantive claim. The closing trilemma in the new §4.2.2 covers this explicitly (if shorthand: rule was not operational in published form → second horn). A second defender objection is that the BOU-already-falsifies hammer is a domain-of-applicability error (rule was registered for 2026, not retroactive baselines). Same trilemma covers this: if the rule's domain was unspecified in advance, the dome concedes the rule was not pre-registered. Both defenses route through the trilemma without lifting the dome out of it.
